description: In Go, prefer established idioms (explicit error wrapping (fmt.Errorf with %w), context propagation, and small interfaces) over generic rewrites.
body: In Go, the ecosystem has settled idioms — explicit error wrapping (fmt.Errorf with %w), context propagation, and small interfaces — and changes that ignore them add risk and review friction. Use when adding or modifying Go code near existing patterns. Do not impose a foreign style or rewrite working code into a generic shape, and avoid abandoning nearby conventions. Actions: read the surrounding code, follow its explicit error wrapping (fmt.Errorf with %w), context propagation, and small interfaces, keep the change minimal, then run go vet, staticcheck, and the race detector (go test -race) and go test ./... -race on the changed package to confirm fit. Validation signals include a small diff consistent with neighbors, a clean lint pass, and passing tests. Risk boundary: a broad idiom migration is its own reviewed task; escalate rather than bundling it into an unrelated change.
